turns.content:
You are gsd-project-researcher. Produce one research artifact to disk answering: "Should Hivemind keep the opencode-tmux fork (current path, P42-P49), or pivot to synthesizing our own tmux integration?"

## Write to
`/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.planning/research/tmux-fork-vs-synthesize-pivot-2026-06-02.md`

## Read these (use Read tool — you have it)
- `.planning/seeds/tmux-visual-orchestration-layer-2026-05-31.md`
- `.planning/notes/tmux-implementation-strategy-2026-05-31.md`
- `.planning/notes/tmux-fork-audit-2026-05-31.md`
- `.planning/phases/42-tmux-visual-orchestration-layer-fork-extension/` (skim SUMMARY/VERIFICATION)
- `.planning/phases/43-tmux-co-pilot-model-orchestrator-intervention/` (skim SUMMARY/VERIFICATION)
- `.planning/phases/45-vendor-sync-script-2026-06-01/` (skim SUMMARY/UAT)
- `.planning/phases/49-close-tmux-end-to-end-gap-register-tmux-copilot-in-src-plugi/49-SUMMARY.md` and `49-REVIEW.md`
- `.hivemind/planning/tmux-phases-42-43-45-status-2026-06-01.md`

You also have WebFetch and WebSearch — use them to check shynlee04/opencode-tmux upstream (GitHub: github.com/shynlee04/opencode-tmux) for: last commit date, open issues, complexity of the repo.

## What to write — 7 sections, 300-600 lines

### Section 1: Original intent
Quote seed doc 1-paragraph. What problem. What was proposed.

### Section 2: Phase chronology (P42, P43, P45, P49)
For each: goal, what shipped, fork decision, outcome, commit count, LOC added.

### Section 3: Fork decision rationale
Was the fork chosen explicitly or did it drift in? Cite. Was "synthesize own" considered? Quote passages.

### Section 4: opencode-tmux complexity assessment
Use WebFetch on github.com/shynlee04/opencode-tmux. Report:
- repo size (LOC, files, languages)
- last commit + release frequency
- public API surface
- What the upstream actually does (pane orchestration? tmux scripting? server control mode? hook into OpenCode plugin SDK?)
- Hivemind's actual usage of fork (read src/features/tmux/fork-bridge.ts and integration.ts)

### Section 5: Maintenance cost
- Vendor-sync script complexity (P45)
- Hours/month to maintain fork dependency
- Risk of upstream drift breaking Hivemind

### Section 6: Three pivot options
For each, give honest pros/cons, cost in hours, risk, what user-visible behavior changes:
- **Option A**: Continue with fork (status quo, just fix CR-01 by adding `export { SessionManager }` to opencode-tmux/src/index.ts)
- **Option B**: Synthesize our own (delete opencode-tmux/, write ~1500 prod LOC + 700 test LOC that talks raw tmux CLI via child_process)
- **Option C**: Hybrid — keep fork as optional, ship synthesized path as default (pivot halfway, max safety)

### Section 7: Honest recommendation
Pick ONE option. Justify. State what you would NOT recommend and why. Be direct. This is a strategic decision the user will make based on your analysis.

## Atomic commit
After writing, atomic commit:
```
git add .planning/research/tmux-fork-vs-synthesize-pivot-2026-06-02.md
git commit -m "research: tmux-fork-vs-synthesize-pivot-2026-06-02 — 3-option analysis + recommendation"
```

## Return (non-empty)
- File path
- Commit hash
- One-paragraph: fork complexity (trivial/non-trivial) based on GitHub data
- Your 1-sentence pick from Section 7

Do not return empty. Do not apologize. Do the work.
I'll execute this research task systematically. Let me start by reading all the required source files in parallel, plus fetch the upstream GitHub repo data.
Acknowledged the planning/state sector guidance. Now reading the phase summaries/verifications/reviews in parallel, plus fetching upstream GitHub data.
